title: Marco Polo and the Silk Road

q.en: While imprisoned in Genoa, who wrote down Marco Polo's stories?
q.zh: 在热那亚坐牢时，是谁记录下马可·波罗的故事？

options.en: Rustichello, a writer and fellow prisoner

explain.en: A fellow prisoner, writer Rustichello, 'took pen in hand to write down every word.'
explain.zh: 同狱的作家鲁斯蒂切洛“拿起笔，记录下每一个字”。

q.en: How long was Marco Polo away from Venice in total?
q.zh: 马可·波罗离开威尼斯总共多久？

explain.en: 'Finally, in 1295, they arrived in Venice. They had been gone for 24 years.'
explain.zh: “终于在1295年，他们抵达威尼斯。他们已经离开24年。”

q.en: What surprised Marco most about China's advances?
q.zh: 中国哪些先进之处最让马可·波罗惊讶？

explain.en: The book says Marco 'was even more impressed to see the Chinese use paper money.'
explain.zh: 书中说马可·波罗“更惊讶地看到中国人使用纸币”。

q.en: Which of these did Marco Polo NOT mention in his book, causing some to doubt him?
q.zh: 以下哪样东西马可·波罗在书中没有提到，导致有人怀疑他？

options.en: The size of Kublai Khan's palace
Tea drinking and the Great Wall

explain.en: Doubters note he made 'no mention... of tea drinking, Chinese writing, and the Great Wall of China.'
explain.zh: 怀疑者指出他“没有提到喝茶、中国文字和万里长城”。

q.en: The book opens with Marco in a prison cell, then tells his life story. This is an example of —
q.zh: 本书以监狱中的马开头，再讲述他的一生。这是——的例子。

explain.en: Starting at the prison and then going back in time is a framing/flashback technique.
explain.zh: 从监狱开始再回溯过往，是一种框架／倒叙手法。

q.en: The author includes a route map and an index. These text features help the reader —
q.zh: 作者加入了路线图和索引。这些文本特征帮助读者——

options.en: Understand the route and find information

explain.en: Maps and an index are organizational features that support understanding and navigation.
explain.zh: 地图和索引是帮助理解与检索信息的组织性特征。

q.en: What connected Europe and China for hundreds of years before Marco Polo?
q.zh: 在马可·波罗之前，是什么把欧洲和中国连接了数百年？

explain.en: Traders brought Chinese goods to Europe for centuries by way of the Silk Road caravan route.
explain.zh: 几百年来，商人通过丝绸之路的商队路线把中国商品带到欧洲。

q.en: Marco Polo's book became known as —
q.zh: 马可·波罗的书后来被称为——

explain.en: It was issued under several titles, 'including The Travels of Marco Polo, which is the title used today.' (Il Milione was Marco's nickname.)
explain.zh: 它以多个书名发行，“其中包括今天所用的《马可·波罗游记》”。（《百万》是马可的绰号，不是书名。）

q.en: What is the author's main purpose?
q.zh: 作者的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To tell the true story of Marco Polo's travels and their history
options.zh: 讲述马可·波罗旅行的真实故事及其历史意义

explain.en: The biography recounts Marco's journey and explains its importance to Europe and history.
explain.zh: 这本传记讲述了马可的旅程，并解释了它对欧洲和历史的重要性。

q.en: How does the author treat the debate about whether Marco Polo told the truth?
q.zh: 关于马可·波罗是否说真话的争论，作者持什么态度？

options.en: Presents both believers and doubters fairly
options.zh: 公平地呈现相信者和怀疑者两方
说只有中国记录才算数

explain.en: The book notes most historians think it is true, while others disagree, and Marco swore he told the truth.
explain.zh: 书中指出多数历史学家认为真实，但也有人反对，而马可至死发誓他说的是真话。

prompt.en: Imagine you are Marco Polo writing a letter home from China. Describe two things you saw that Europeans had never seen (like paper money or coal) and why they would be amazed.
prompt.zh: 想象你是马可·波罗，从中国给家里写信。描述两件欧洲人从未见过的东西（如纸币或煤），并说明他们为何会惊讶。

en: Pick two real details from the book.
Explain what each item is and how it was used in China.
Connect it to why Europeans at the time would be surprised.
zh: 从书中选取两个真实的细节。
解释每件物品是什么、在中国如何被使用。
联系说明当时的欧洲人为什么会惊讶。
